Joining a Competition

After a competition has been created, other players can be invited to join in using messaging. Your title can also enable players to search for available competitions by invoking the XOnlineCompetitionSearch function. When searching for an available competition, your title sets the XOnlineCompetitionSearch function's dwQueryID parameter to the ID of the Available Competitions query generated by XLAST.

During the competition registration period, players who want to take part in a competition must register for it. To register a player, your title must call XOnlineCompetitionManageEntrant. Your game can show players all competitions they have registered for by calling XOnlineCompetitionSearch and passing the ID of the My Competitions query generated by XLAST as the value for the dwQueryID parameter.

If the player has been explicitly invited to join a competition, your title should set the XOnlineCompetitionManageEntrant function's dwAction parameter to XONLINE_ACTION_JOIN_PRIVATE. For all others, your title should set the dwAction parameter to XONLINE_ACTION_JOIN.

When the registration period ends, the service automatically performs event slotting to initialize the first round events if enough players have registered. If the minimum number of players have not registered for the competition, the competition instance is canceled, then deleted 48 hours after close of registration.

If the competition was not canceled and slotting has taken place, the competition becomes "active." This is indicated by the fact that the XONLINE_COMP_ATTR_STATUS attribute in the Competitions target table contains the value XONLINE_COMP_STATUS_ACTIVE. If a competition is active, players may play through each event match as the matches occur.
